channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Bluesky is getting ‘communities’
item.description: Bluesky will be getting "communities," which will function as smaller spaces where you can "go deeper and hang out with people who care about the same stuff" sometime this year, according to head of product Alex Benzer. They will be built on the decentralized AT Protocol that underpins Bluesky, with Benzer saying that "it's a […]

item.title: A knee-jerk reaction or something more? Nvidia's market cap dropped by almost $330 billion in 24 hours as the AI giant reeled from Broadcom's poor guidance
item.description: Nvidia saw its market cap tank by $330 billion after Broadcom delivered guidance that many AI investors considered disappointing amid lofty expectations.

item.title: I’m excited for Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time on the Switch 2, but I wish it was a sequel to Majora’s Mask instead
item.description: Instead of a fourth Ocarina of Time re-release I want a sequel to the N64 classic, or at least as well.

item.title: Windows 11 sucks slightly less now, thanks to a June update
item.description: The update brings a low-latency profile, speeds up search, and patches hundreds of flaws.

item.title: ‘A fully secured critical site in 2030 does not look like a single technology’: Why drone visibility and accountability are just as important as security controls
item.description: Critical national infrastructure is at risk – layered detection, persistent visibility and clear accountability for all drones is now key.

item.title: Surprise upset: GPT-5.5 beats Claude Fable 5 on brutal new Agents’ Last Exam benchmark
item.description: Researchers from the University of California, Berkeley's Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence (RDI), alongside an advisory committee of over 300 domain experts, have launched Agents’ Last Exam (ALE)—a grueling new benchmark built to measure whether artificial intelligence can actually execute economically valuable, long-horizon professional workflows.In a shocking upset, OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 from April, operating through the Codex harness, secured the absolute top spot on the new ALE Leaderboard with a 24.0% pass rate, beating Anthropic's highly anticipated, brand new Mythos-class C...

item.title: Framework delays its first Laptop 13 Pro shipments by a month
item.description: The Framework Laptop 13 Pro is delayed. The new 13-inch Framework flagship was set to launch in June, but shipments from the first batch are now expected in July - and there's still a chance some shipments could slip to early August. If you're not in the first batch, your Laptop 13 Pro shifts from […]

item.title: Xbox CEO says current margins 'cannot continue' in public letter to staff
item.description: The more things change, the more they stay the same.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Thursday, June 11 (game #1599)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Thursday, June 11 (game #1096)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Thursday, June 11 (game #830)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: I test a lot of espresso machines, but the new KitchenAid KF4 has one of the smartest features I've ever seen — and it'll stop your coffee maker from getting gross
item.description: Automatic coffee makers can get pretty gross, but KitchenAid's latest model can evaporate all the water inside to keep itself fresh.

item.title: Apple’s new Siri AI knows when to shut up
item.description: Apple's new Siri AI is finally here, and so far, it seems like it works. I have access and have been messing around with it, and my biggest impression so far is that Siri AI is quite curt - which I mean as a compliment. Many AI chatbots are cheery and wordy. While a more […]

item.title: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims
item.description: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX, alleging he was fired for raising AI safety concerns about Grok days before SpaceX's historic IPO.

item.title: Canada announces bill banning social media for anyone under 16
item.description: The regulation also imposes new safety expectations on 'AI chatbot services.'

item.title: Why Andrew Yang is building instead of waiting for Washington
item.description: Andrew Yang’s 2020 presidential campaign was based on a warning that automation and AI would hollow out the labor market and concentrate wealth in the hands of a few. At the time, ideas like Universal Basic Income felt fringe. Now Dario Amodei, Sam Altman, and Bernie Sanders are all saying versions of the same thing. An entrepreneur at heart, […]
